text:
Aspen sat on her porch, nursing a mug of ale while watching the sun dip below the mountains. There was an unbroken stretch of green before her, stretching forever. She sighed. It was boring. She wanted adventure.

"The Moonstone" waited unread on her bookshelf. A classic. Maybe it could stir some excitement in her. She flipped through the pages. It was a tiresome tale about a diamond.

"Hmm...never could get into those novels."

She stood, her feet tapping lightly against the worn wooden floors.

Out there stood the forest. She could almost smell its pine.

"Certainly don't have time for that." She shrugged. "Would probably ruin my dress."

She shrugged again. Then a sound pierced the stillness. It was like birdsong but haunting. Her curiosity piqued. 

She tiptoed outside, stepping lightly over broken branches.

There. That strange tune was coming from ahead.

She approached the clearing. There stood a stone cottage
